name: Lamivudine reduced tauopathy pathology and behavioral deficits in mice
summary: A disease-model mouse signal supports neurodegeneration relevance for 3TC but remains indirect for broad aging.
statement: In P301S tau transgenic mice, lamivudine reduced tau phosphorylation, inflammation, neuronal death, and hippocampal atrophy, and attenuated motor and short-term-memory deficits.
time_horizon: Lamivudine administered through drinking water in a tauopathy disease model
quantitative_note: The source reports reduced pathology and improved rotarod and Y-maze performance, but this is not a normal-aging trial.
caveats: The model is tauopathy-specific rather than broad aging.
The evidence is preclinical and does not establish human cognitive benefit.
Attribution to retrotransposon suppression must be separated from broader drug effects in future work.
